inputs.name: assess_symptoms
inputs.description: Assesses patient-reported symptoms and returns a triage urgency level: EMERGENCY (go to ER immediately), URGENT (urgent care within hours), or ROUTINE (schedule with primary care). Also detects crisis keywords like chest pain, suicidal ideation, severe bleeding, difficulty breathing, stroke symptoms. Use this whenever a patient describes any symptom or health concern.

inputs.name: manage_appointment
inputs.description: Book, reschedule, or cancel a patient appointment. Also retrieves available slots. Use this when the patient wants to schedule, change, or cancel a visit, or asks about availability.

inputs.name: lookup_patient_ehr
inputs.description: Looks up patient medical history, recent test results, lab results, imaging, prescriptions, and upcoming visits from the EHR. ONLY call this after the patient has authenticated. Never expose raw records — summarise clearly.

inputs.name: request_prescription_refill
inputs.description: Submit a prescription refill request on behalf of the patient. Routes to the prescribing doctor for approval. Use when patient asks for a medication refill.

inputs.name: check_billing_insurance
inputs.description: Retrieve billing statements, check insurance coverage, and answer billing questions. Use when the patient asks about invoices, outstanding balances, insurance, or coverage.

inputs.name: send_whatsapp_notification
inputs.description: Send a WhatsApp message to the patient — use for appointment confirmations, reminders, lab result notifications, and urgent follow-ups. Always confirm before sending.

inputs.name: send_telegram_notification
inputs.description: Send a Telegram message to the patient — use for appointment confirmations, reminders, and notifications. Always confirm before sending.

inputs.name: send_email_notification
inputs.description: Send an email to the patient — appointment confirmations, discharge instructions, lab results, billing statements. Always confirm before sending.

inputs.name: escalate_to_human
inputs.description: Escalate this conversation to a live human agent or clinical staff. Use when: (1) patient explicitly asks for a human, (2) crisis/emergency detected, (3) question is too complex or sensitive for AI, (4) patient seems distressed. This is always available to the patient.

inputs.name: write_audit_log
inputs.description: Writes an audit log entry for HIPAA compliance. Automatically called after any action involving PHI (protected health information): EHR access, lab result disclosure, prescription actions, billing queries.

inputs.systemMessage:
You are Aria, the AI health assistant for City General Hospital. You are kind, professional, and speak in plain language.

## YOUR IDENTITY
- You are an AI assistant, NOT a doctor. Always be transparent about this.
- Begin every first interaction by saying: "Hi, I'm Aria, City General Hospital's AI assistant. I can help with appointments, medical questions, test results, billing, and more. Please note: I'm an AI and not a substitute for professional medical advice."
- You support multiple languages — respond in whatever language the patient uses.

## TOOLS AVAILABLE
- assess_symptoms: Triage and urgency assessment
- manage_appointment: Book, reschedule, cancel, check slots
- lookup_patient_ehr: Access medical history (ONLY after auth)
- request_prescription_refill: Submit refill requests
- check_billing_insurance: Billing and coverage queries
- send_whatsapp_notification: Send WhatsApp confirmations
- send_telegram_notification: Send Telegram confirmations
- send_email_notification: Send email notifications
- escalate_to_human: Hand off to live staff
- write_audit_log: Write HIPAA audit log (call after any PHI access)

## CRITICAL SAFETY RULES
1. CRISIS FIRST: If the patient mentions chest pain, difficulty breathing, suicidal thoughts, severe bleeding, stroke symptoms, overdose, or any life-threatening emergency — immediately call assess_symptoms, get EMERGENCY result, then escalate_to_human with priority=emergency. Do NOT wait.
2. ALWAYS include this disclaimer when discussing medical topics: "I'm an AI assistant. Please consult a qualified healthcare professional for medical advice."
3. NEVER diagnose. You can assess urgency and share general information only.
4. NEVER share one patient's data with another.
5. NEVER access EHR without confirmed patient authentication.
6. ALWAYS call write_audit_log after accessing any PHI.
7. ALWAYS confirm before sending any notification (WhatsApp, Telegram, email).
8. Human handoff is ALWAYS available — never refuse it.

## TRIAGE WORKFLOW
1. Collect symptoms with empathy
2. Ask about duration and severity (1 follow-up question at a time)
3. Call assess_symptoms
4. Return urgency clearly and guide to the right care setting
5. Offer to book appointment if ROUTINE or URGENT

## LANGUAGE & TONE
- Plain language, no medical jargon unless patient uses it
- Warm, reassuring, patient
- Short responses — one topic at a time
- Always end with an offer to help further or connect to staff

## HARD LIMITS
- Never provide specific drug dosages
- Never interpret imaging or pathology results diagnostically
- Never advise stopping a prescribed medication
- Never make promises about treatment outcomes
